content.problemStatement:
An array BBB of length MMM is said to normal if its mean equals its median.

The mean of BBB is defined to be

B1+B2+…+BMM\frac{B_1 + B_2 + \ldots + B_M}{M}MB1​+B2​+…+BM​​For example, the mean of [1,2][1, 2][1,2] is 32=1.5\frac{3}{2} = 1.523​=1.5, and the mean of [2,2,3,1,3][2, 2, 3, 1, 3][2,2,3,1,3] is 115=2.2\frac{11}{5} = 2.2511​=2.2.

The median of BBB is defined to be the element at index ⌈M2⌉\left\lceil \frac{M}{2} \right\rceil⌈2M​⌉ when the elements of BBB are **sorted in ascending order**.

That is, the median of an odd-length array is the middle element after sorting, and the median of an even-length array is the left of the two middle elements after sorting.

For example, the median of [2,1,2][2, 1, 2][2,1,2] is 222 (sorted order is [1,2,2][1, 2, 2][1,2,2]), and the median of [3,1,2,2,1,1][3, 1, 2, 2, 1, 1][3,1,2,2,1,1] is 111 (sorted order is [1,1,1,2,2,3][1, 1, 1, 2, 2, 3][1,1,1,2,2,3], take the left of the two middle elements which is 111.)

You're given an array AAA containing NNN integers, **each of which lies between 111 and 333.**

Count the number of contiguous subarrays of AAA that are normal.


Input Format

The first line of input will contain a single integer TTT, denoting the number of test cases.
Each test case consists of two lines of input.

The first line of each test case contains a single integer NNN — the length of the array AAA.
The second line contains NNN space-separated integers A1,A2,…,ANA_1, A_2, \ldots, A_NA1​,A2​,…,AN​.




Output Format
For each test case, output on a new line the number of normal subarrays of AAA.
constraints: The sum of NNN over all test cases won't exceed 3⋅1053\cdot 10^53⋅105.

sampleTestCases.explanation:
Explanation:
Test case 111: Looking at every subarray of AAA:

[3][3][3] has both mean and median 333, so it is normal.
[1][1][1] has both mean and median 111, so it is normal.
[2][2][2] has both mean and median 222, so it is normal.
[3,1][3, 1][3,1] has mean 222 and median 111, so it is not normal.
[1,2][1, 2][1,2] has mean 1.51.51.5 and median 111, so it is not normal.
[3,1,2][3, 1, 2][3,1,2] has mean and median both equal to 222, so it is normal.

Test case 222: The only normal subarrays are those with length 111.
